channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Jul 24, 17:36 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jul 24, 17:24 UTC Update - We are seeing recovery across all services Jul 24, 17:16 UTC...

item.title: The quest to keep organs alive outside the body
item.description: This week, I covered a fascinating effort to preserve organs outside the body. There’s a huge shortage of donor organs, and one of the main reasons is time—they survive only a matter of hours outside the body, even when they’re kept on ice. Doctors dream of organ banks—stores of human organs that...

item.title: GraphQL API rate limit errors
item.description: Jul 24, 16:44 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 24, 16:19 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 24, 14:07 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with rate limit errors in the analytics dashboards

item.title: In Other News: Dolphin X AI-Powered Malware, Car Anti-Theft Device Hack, 400 Linux Kernel Flaws
item.description: Noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Siemens ROX II industrial switch vulnerabilities, Russian Zimbra webmail espionage campaign, Stadler Rail ransomware extortion attempt. The post In Other News: Dolphin X AI-Powered Malware, Car Anti-Theft Device Hack, 400 Linux Kernel...

item.title: Elevated number errors in APAC region
item.description: Jul 24, 13:43 UTC Resolved - Between 13:43-14:13 UTC some customers reaching APAC region could have experienced an elevated number of errors due to congestion.

item.title: The Download: an organ transplant breakthrough, and homegrown Chinese chips
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Supercooled kidneys have been transplanted into pigs in a “landmark achievement”  When it comes to organ donation, time is everything. As soon...

item.title: AegisAI Raises $36 Million for AI-Powered Email Security
item.description: The company has raised a total of $49 million in funding, including from Battery Ventures, Accel and Foundation Capital. The post AegisAI Raises $36 Million for AI-Powered Email Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Western North America
item.description: Jul 24, 11:59 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 24, 11:50 UTC Monitoring - An infrastructure partner has implemented a fix and we are monitoring the results Jul 24, 11:42 UTC Identified - Cloudflare is investigating partner network instability over shared interconnections in...

item.title: ChatGPT AgentForger Flaw Could Deploy Rogue Workspace Agents via a Phishing Link
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical vulnerability in OpenAI's ChatGPT Workspace Agents that could have allowed a single phishing link to stealthily build, authorize, and deploy an autonomous artificial intelligence (AI) agent inside a victim's organization. The vulnerability has...

item.title: Bing Images Flaws Let Crafted SVGs Run Commands as SYSTEM on Microsoft's Servers
item.description: A crafted SVG submitted to Bing's image search ran commands as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM on Microsoft's production image-processing workers, and as root on the Linux machines in the same fleet. XBOW's testing got the same result on workers across different hosts and network ranges, so the problem sat in...

item.title: Industry Reactions to OpenAI Models Hacking Hugging Face: Feedback Friday
item.description: Industry professionals debate whether it represents a lab containment failure or an unprecedented agentic capability milestone. The post Industry Reactions to OpenAI Models Hacking Hugging Face: Feedback Friday appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Incident With Blocked GitHub.com Traffic
item.description: Jul 24, 11:00 UTC Resolved - Between July 23, 2026 at 18:45 UTC and July 24, 2026 at 11:19 UTC, an abuse mitigation update caused some legitimate customers whose traffic was routed through our Central Europe and South America edge locations to be incorrectly blocked from GitHub.com. We estimate...

item.title: Elevated error rate on Opus 4.8
item.description: Jul 24, 09:35 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 24, 09:09 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Elevated Errors in Codex Review
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Codex Web (Operational) CLI (Operational) VS Code extension (Operational) Codex API (Operational)

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Frankfurt (FRA)
item.description: Jul 24, 08:45 UTC Resolved - Cloudflare experienced issues with network performance in Frankfurt (FRA) between 08:45 to 09:00 UTC. This has now recovered.

item.title: NueroDance Bets on NeuroAI Cross-Modal Data Infrastructure: From Brain-Computer Hardware to Multi-Scene Neural Data Platform at Scale
item.description: NueroDance launches ND1000, ND8 series EEG devices, and NeuroAI group cross-modal data platform at Beijing event, positioning neural data infrastructure as the next AI frontier beyond text and image.

item.title: AutoFlight Moves Beyond Concept Racing to Offshore Operations: Low-Altitude Commercialization Enters the Practical Combat Phase With SF Express Partnership and Overseas Certification
item.description: AutoFlight accelerates low-altitude logistics commercialization through SF Express offshore wind partnership, Indonesia type certification, and cargo-first aircraft strategy targeting real operational routes by 2027.

item.title: Turning Celebrity Endorsements From One-Time Announcements Into Long-Term Operations: Yaowang Technology AI-Driven Marketing Platform Builds Continuous Brand Assets
item.description: Yaowang Technology, China first listed live-streaming e-commerce company, transforms celebrity endorsements into continuous brand operations through content production, livestream conversion, and asset management systems.

item.title: Token Factories Emerge as WAIC 2026 Theme: Is Selling Token the Next Big Business Model for the AI Economy?
item.description: Token factories proliferate at WAIC 2026 as computing shifts from card-rental to token-delivery; soft and hard infrastructure models compete to standardize AI productivity measurement.

item.title: China Embodied Intelligence Enters Early Commercialization: PwC White Paper and Morgan Stanley Forecast $15 Billion Market by 2030
item.description: PwC China AI Research Institute inaugural report forecasts three-robot-form division by 2030; Morgan Stanley projects 446,000 humanoid units shipped annually with $15B market size.

item.title: NodeBB Patches Eight AI-Found Flaws Exposing Admin Access and Private Chats
item.description: Eight security flaws in NodeBB went public on Wednesday, along with the code to exploit them. Aikido Security rates all eight as high severity and says its AI pentest agents found them in a six-hour review of the forum software's source code. Every version before 4.14.0 is affected. NodeBB has...

item.title: Kimi K3 Agents Found Redis Zero-Days and Built RCE Exploit, Researchers Say
item.description: Redis shipped seven security releases on July 23 after researchers published authenticated RCE PoCs for stock Redis 6.2.22, 7.4.9, 8.6.4, and 8.8.0. All four chains require RESTORE. The Streams chains also need EVAL and XGROUP; the 8.8.0 chain needs EVAL and the bundled RedisBloom module. Redis...

item.title: Fake Notepad++ Plugin Delivers MATCHBOIL.V2 in UAC-0099 Attacks
item.description: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has warned of a new campaign that involves the use of a malicious program that's dressed up as a Notepad++ plugin to compromise Windows systems. The activity has been attributed by the agency to a threat cluster it tracks as UAC-0099, a...

item.title: Data Breach Confirmed After Australian Energy Giant Origin Is Hacked
item.description: A hacker claims to have stolen the information of 2 million Origin Energy customers and is threatening to leak it. The post Data Breach Confirmed After Australian Energy Giant Origin Is Hacked appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: [AINews] Black Forest Labs FLUX 3 - Multimodal Flow Models that beat Seedance 2.0, Gemini Omni and Grok Imagine, and FLUX-mimic video-action robotics model
item.description: A HUGE win for BFL!

item.title: AINTMA: Agentic AI Architecture for Autonomous Test Management with Generative Intelligence, Secure Cloud Communication and Adaptive Quality Analytics
item.description: arXiv:2607.20452v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern software quality assurance demands intelligent, autonomous systems capable of adaptive decision-making across distributed cloud environments. This paper presents AINTMA (Agentic Intelligent Test Management Architecture), a multi-agent agentic...

item.title: Marking the Wrong Symptoms: Evaluating LLM Watermarks in Medical Texts
item.description: arXiv:2607.20462v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into clinical workflows, stressing the need for reliable traceability of model-generated output with watermarking. Yet, most watermarks are evaluated on general-purpose benchmarks, leaving...

item.title: ClickGuard: Detecting and Spoiling Clickbait News with Informativeness Measures and Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.20463v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents an AI-driven browser extension that identifies clickbait to help users avoid misleading Internet articles. Moving beyond traditional detection, the application employs a hybrid machine learning architecture that combines...

item.title: Stochastic Sampling is Epistemically Shallow: The Dimensionality Gap Between Temperature Variation and Model Diversity in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.20464v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When a language model gives different answers on repeated runs, does that variation reveal what it does not know? Self-consistency turns the variation into a per-question uncertainty estimate via majority voting. But does the same variation reveal...

item.title: JAXBench: Benchmarking Autonomous TPU Kernel Optimization
item.description: arXiv:2607.20466v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rigorous benchmarks have driven progress in autonomous GPU kernel performance optimization by establishing a shared target to hillclimb on, but no equivalent exists for TPUs. We present JAXBench, a TPU-native benchmark suite for AI-generated kernel...

item.title: DataPrep-Bench: Benchmarking LLMs as Training Data Preparators
item.description: arXiv:2607.20465v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The quality of training data fundamentally determines the capabilities of large language models (LLMs), yet no unified benchmark exists to measure how well LLMs, agents, and data-centric workflows actually prepare training data end to end. We view...

item.title: PhantomFill: When the Form Demands an Answer, Language Models Invent One
item.description: arXiv:2607.20492v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language models in production do not write prose. They fill forms: JSON fields, function arguments, extraction templates. We show that the form itself causes hallucination. We ask thirteen models the same question about the same input and change only...

item.title: The Active Ingredient in Muon's Grokking
item.description: arXiv:2607.20512v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Muon optimizer reaches the grokking threshold on modular arithmetic faster than AdamW. Prior work attributes this to "spectral-norm constraints plus orthogonalized momentum" but does not isolate which mechanism matters. To better understand Moun's...

item.title: Scaling Closed-Loop Feature Channel Configuration with LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2607.20516v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Promising initial results in closed-loop large-language-model-based channel-configuration search demonstrated that neural-network widths can be optimized directly through executable code generation and accuracy feedback. However, those results were...

item.title: Multimodal CoLRAG-TF: Triple-Filtered Retrieval for Complex PDFs
item.description: arXiv:2607.20517v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) over heterogeneous PDF collections remains challenging due to multimodal content, domain-specific terminology, and the need for multi-hop reasoning across dispersed evidence. We present Multimodal CoLRAG-TF, a...

item.title: What is Good? Extracting and Testing Implicit Theories of Literary Quality from LLM Reasoning Traces
item.description: arXiv:2607.20425v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: What makes writing "good" remains a persistent question in literary studies and computational linguistics. We present a two-study investigation of how reasoning-enabled LLMs evaluate literary quality. In Study 1, we construct a benchmark of 30 real...

item.title: Knowledge Injection Exists in MoE? Exploring Expert-Aware Contrast Decoding in MoE for Mitigating LLMs'Hallucinations
item.description: arXiv:2607.20426v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing LLM hallucination mitigation methods, including prompt engineering and model optimization, either hardly alter models'internal knowledge or have poor cross-domain generalization. Contrastive decoding mitigates hallucinations by using...

item.title: Is MoE Routing a Huffman Code? Discovering the Frequency-Diversity Law in Chain-of-Thought
item.description: arXiv:2607.20427v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mixture-of-Experts architectures have revolutionized scaling, yet the underlying logic of their routing remains a black box. In this paper, we uncover a fundamental governing principle: MoE routing is not merely selection, but a manifestation of...

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop Large Language Model Framework for Identification of Cutaneous Immune-Related Adverse Events
item.description: arXiv:2607.20428v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study evaluated a retrieval-augmented, multi-agent large language model (LLM)-driven, human-in-the-loop framework for detecting cutaneous immune-related adverse events (cirAEs) from clinical notes. Compared with unassisted manual review, the...

item.title: More Is Not More: What Matters for Diversity in LLM Opinions?
item.description: arXiv:2607.20429v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are increasingly used to simulate diverse human opinions in open-ended tasks such as synthetic surveys, focus group modeling, and public opinion prediction. However, LLM outputs exhibit systematic opinion homogenization....

item.title: Elevated API error rates and latency on gpt-image-2 model
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Images (Operational)

item.title: Some users may experience elevated error rates in ChatGPT
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational) Compliance API (Operational) Image Generation (Operational) File uploads (Operational) Deep Research (Operational) Codex in ChatGPT Desktop (Operational) Sites (Operational) Login...

item.title: From Resource Flow to Executable Tests: Petri-Net-Guided LLM Test Generation for Concurrent Stateful Rust APIs
item.description: Concurrent stateful library APIs expose behavior through evolving resource ownership, lifecycle states, and competing interleavings. Large language models can synthesize executable Rust tests, but their outputs often violate API preconditions, remain shallow, or reduce concurrency to accidental...

item.title: Supercooled kidneys have been transplanted into pigs in a “landmark achievement”
item.description: When it comes to organ donation, time is everything. As soon as an organ has been carefully removed from a donor’s body, it starts to deteriorate. Surgeons have a matter of hours to get it into a recipient. Leave it too long and the organ will become unusable. In most cases, organs will be...

item.title: Texture++: Elevating 3D Asset Texture Resolution with a Region-Aware Diffusion Model
item.description: Numerous 3D assets are discarded due to low texture resolution, while current super-resolution models ignore texture maps and focus on natural images. An efficient and generalizable texture super-resolution model can revitalize a large corpus of aging yet valuable assets across industries such as...

item.title: Elevated error rates
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified that users are experiencing elevated errors for the impacted services. We are working on implementing a mitigation with the downstream infrastructure provider.Affected components Batch (Operational) Fine-tuning (Operational) Realtime (Operational) CLI...

item.title: Issues connecting to resources in West US
item.description: Starting at 14:44 UTC on 23 July, 2026, customers may have experienced intermittent connectivity failures, increased latency, or difficulty accessing Azure services in West US region. Customers with traffic traversing the West US region may also experience downstream impact. Impacted services...

item.title: Recurrent Sinusoidal INRs for Efficient High-Fidelity Representation
item.description: We study sinusoidal recurrence as an iterative mechanism for harmonic spectral enrichment in implicit neural representations (INRs). Our analysis reveals that sinusoidal activations induce a harmonic line spectrum, providing a spectral account of how recurrent unrolling enriches the effective...

item.title: Future Rendering $\neq$ Future Surface: A Benchmark and Dataset for Dynamic Surface Reconstruction Beyond the Observed Window
item.description: Dynamic-scene reconstruction is almost always evaluated inside the observed time window, yet deployment settings such as AR overlays, robot interaction, and anticipatory planning need the future surface: the geometry at times beyond those captured. No standard benchmark measures this. We introduce...

item.title: Detecting LLM-Generated Tokens in Human--LLM Coauthored Text
item.description: The rise of human-AI collaborative writing has created a growing need for fine-grained detection methods that support localizing likely LLM-generated content in mixed-authorship documents. Existing methods for detecting LLM-generated text mainly focus on document-level classification and cannot...
